The Ccapa family entered specialty coffee in recent years; a balanced washed Typica with dark berry, plum, cacao and dried fruit (48h ferment).

farms.name: Joaquin Lipa (Santa Juana)
farms.note: Finca Santa Juana, Inambari, Puno, Peru. Joaquin Coaquira Lipa has made coffee his family's main income, tending coffee plants and the bee hives that, in an ancestral practice taught by his parents, improve flowering and fruiting. A full-bodied washed Bourbon — Malbec grape, currant, pluot, blueberry, cacao.

farms.name: Lisbeth Mariño
farms.note: Pacaybamba, Cusco, Peru. Lisbeth Mariño trained as a cup taster and is now a leading woman in her district, providing technical guidance to other producers. Her double-fermented washed SL9 ("Gesha Inca") is intricate and floral — iris, pineberry, mango, peach and juicy citrus, best enjoyed neat.

farms.name: Roberto Palomino Flores (La Rinconada)
farms.note: Finca La Rinconada, Cusco, Peru. Roberto Carlos Palomino Flores, a 25-year-old agricultural technician, shares knowledge with neighbours and is building an expansive nursery. His washed Gesha (18h + 36h dry ferment) is opulent and berry-forward — raspberry, Fuji apple, cherry with lavender, rose and honeysuckle.

farms.name: Manuel Marlo (Pacpa)
farms.note: Finca Pacpa, Lonya Grande, Amazonas, Peru. A young producer building on his family's coffee legacy, working direct with Flowerchild. His washed Gesha is loud and layered — blackberry, Autumn Royal grape, plum, raspberry and violet florals with a long, punchy acidity.

farms.name: Abel Salinas (Arashi)
farms.note: Finca Arashi, Saraguro, Loja, Ecuador. Abel Salinas planted his first trees in 2016 and now has 22,000+; a Cup of Excellence winner in Ecuador renowned for processing consistency despite climate swings. His washed Mejorado is syrupy and clean — stone fruit, honeycomb, pluot, orange zest.
farms.notable: A Cup of Excellence winner in Ecuador, known for processing consistency.

farms.name: Miguel Dota
farms.note: Saraguro, Loja, Ecuador. The Dota family each tend small garden plots; Miguel's tiny Sidra lot is among the most celebrated Ecuadorian coffees Flowerchild has offered — big yet delicate, with an umami undercurrent, marionberry, blood orange, vanilla, lemon meringue and a sharp citric structure.
farms.notable: Miguel's tiny Sidra lot is among the most celebrated Ecuadorian coffees Flowerchild has offered — big yet delicate, with an umami.

farms.name: Carlos Mario
farms.note: Pitalito, Huila, Colombia. Carlos Mario has grown his operation over 15 years to two farms, working with his wife Patricia and uncle Hermes plus seasonal pickers. His washed Pink Bourbon (24h in cherry, 60h ferment) is lively with an almost Kenyan acidity — guava, raspberry, melon, stone fruit, red apple.

farms.name: Café Ticuna
farms.note: Garzón, Huila, Colombia. A coffee project co-founded by importers José Jadir Losada and Kyle Bellinger, spanning three farms in Garzón plus El Mirador in Suaza. Unusually for Colombia they grow Kenyan SL-28 and the rare large-bean Caturrón (a Timor-hybrid × Bourbon/Caturra cross, related to Variedad Colombia) — both washed, clean and distinctive.

farms.name: Reinel (El Espejo)
farms.note: Finca El Espejo, Santa María, Huila, Colombia. Reinel Borbón is a widely respected 50-year-old second-generation grower on a 4-ha, ~13,000-tree farm, with his eldest son now learning the family processing recipe. His washed Pink Bourbon (20h in-cherry + 48h open-tank ferment) is clean and bright — red grape, citrus, raspberry, pomegranate, melon.

farms.name: Victor Bonilla (La Chorrera)
farms.note: Finca La Chorrera, La Argentina, Huila, Colombia. Víctor Alfonso Bonilla, a Q-grader and third-generation grower, dedicates his 3.5-ha, 5,500-tree farm entirely to the rare Bourbon Aruzi, which he discovered and has championed to competition success. A balanced, buttery washed cup — black cherry, chocolate, date, dried fruit.

farms.name: Sergio Caro (Los Pinos)
farms.note: Caicedo, Antioquia, Colombia. Sergio Caro fell for coffee while working at a cooperative in Urrao and planted Chiroso; one of his first viable harvests reached the finals of the Colombian Cup of Excellence. A big, silky washed Chiroso at 2,300m+ — black plum, dark berries, grape, nectarine, lavender syrup and pear.
farms.notable: Reached the finals of the Colombian Cup of Excellence with an early harvest.

farms.name: Andrés Felipe Osorio (Finca El Nogal)
farms.note: Finca El Nogal, Santa María, Huila, Colombia. Andrés Felipe Osorio, a 20-year-old with already five years' experience, focuses his 11-ha, 11,000-tree farm exclusively on Pink Bourbon. A tropical, juicy washed Rosado — mandarin, lemon drop, pineapple, ripe stone fruit, heavy body.

farms.name: José Martínez (Finca El Casino)
farms.note: Finca El Casino, Palestina, Huila, Colombia. José Elcias Martínez and his wife Francy have farmed for over 20 years, continuing a family legacy (and famously home to dozens of rescued dogs). A big-bodied washed Pink Bourbon — prickly pear, citrus zest, candied grape, tropical fruit.

farms.name: Wilson Alba
farms.note: Palestina, Huila, Colombia. Don Wilson Alba Córdoba, a third-generation farmer, has grown his projects to ~13 ha across Fincas Sierra Morena and El Cedro, employing 20 local pickers at harvest. His fully washed Pink Bourbon (15h in-cherry ferment) is a perennial favourite — ruby grapefruit, raspberry, red currant, lemon and delicate florals.

farms.name: Nemesio Ramos (Galaxia)
farms.note: Finca Galaxia, Vereda El Carmen, Tarqui, Huila, Colombia. Nemesio Ramos Trujillo, a third-generation grower of 35 years, farms ~3 ha high on the Cordillera Central with his wife and six children. An award winner for his Caturra; this washed lot blends Caturra with his floral Pink Bourbon for balance — apricot, papaya, creamy citrus, Rainier cherry.
farms.notable: A Colombian award winner recognised for his exceptional Caturra.

farms.name: Martha Cartagena (Hoyo Rico)
farms.note: Inzá, Cauca, Colombia. Martha Cartagena and her husband Arnulfo are building a new farm, Hoyo Rico, high in the mountains; Martha handles pricing, delivery scheduling and oversees drying. An elegant washed Chiroso — yellow cherry plum, mandarin, stone fruit, berry tea, round and juicy.

farms.name: Santiago Caro (La Falda)
farms.note: Urrao, Antioquia, Colombia. Santiago Caro Bermúdez is a young producer and a quality analyst with Coffee Quest; his father bet on planting Chiroso a decade ago. A clean, comforting washed Chiroso — sugar cane, marshmallow-soft body, red cherry, candied grape, pear, light citrus.

farms.name: Servio Botina (El Cedro)
farms.note: Finca El Cedro, San Agustín, Huila, Colombia. Servio Tulio Botina Quinayas produces one of the most dynamic washed Pink Bourbon lots Flowerchild has seen — deliberately veering from the region's pulpy style toward clarity. Pluot, strawberry, currant, cranberry, with a structure that firms into a citric acidity as it cools.

farms.name: Gilberto Chilito (Villa Caney)
farms.note: Llanada de Naranjos, San Agustín, Huila, Colombia. Finca Villa Caney — Gilberto Chilito has built coffee operations since age 15 and farms ~2 ha (1,300 Gesha trees) with his wife Marta and three children. A big, tropical late-harvest washed Gesha — raspberry, guava, passion fruit, blood orange and long florals.

farms.name: Victor Pineda (La Cenovia)
farms.note: Finca La Cenovia, Caicedo, Antioquia, Colombia. Víctor Pineda has processed coffee for five years and recently planted Geisha and Chiroso as his step into specialty. A dense, well-structured washed Chiroso — apricot, honey, lemongrass, melon, Rainier cherry with a long honey finish.

farms.name: Faver Ninco
farms.note: Finca Costa Rica, Santa María, Huila, Colombia. Faver Ninco learned coffee from his father and neighbours ~25 years ago and turned to specialty varietals; known for an unusually sweet expression of Gesha. Washed — purple grape, white nectarine, pineapple, blood orange and long jasmine/coffee-blossom florals.

farms.name: Jhon Bermudez (La Estrella)
farms.note: Finca La Estrella, Urrao, Antioquia, Colombia. Jhon Alexander Bermudez, a second-generation grower, farms ~3 ha (11,000 trees) of the now sought-after Chiroso. An approachable washed cup — apple, cinnamon, caramel, bright and dried stone fruit with a light body and delicate florality.

farms.name: Ernedis Rodriguez (El Paraiso, Gigante)
farms.note: Gigante, Huila, Colombia. Ernedis Rodriguez farms a Colombian "El Paraiso" (entirely separate from Diego Bermúdez's El Paraíso in Cauca), producing washed Sidra, Caturra and field-blend lots of notable clarity.

farms.name: Julio Alberto (El Tabor)
farms.note: Finca El Tabor, El Tabor, San Agustín, Huila, Colombia. Julio Alberto Muñoz Ordoñez has grown coffee his whole life (52 years) across a 15-ha farm with five varieties. His washed Borbón Rosado (1 day in cherry + 2-day sealed ferment) is intensely fruity — red tea, grape, rose, sticky-sweet fruit, hibiscus.

farms.name: Jose Ever Muñoz (Finca La Esperanza, Suaza)
farms.note: Bajo Horizonte, Suaza, Huila, Colombia. José Ever Muñoz, farming since 2012 with his wife Fernanda, won the regional Copa de Oro with this coffee and is a member of the Cooperativa Divino Niño del Horizonte. A honey-processed Pink Bourbon — papaya, honeysuckle, melon, yellow cherry, mango, syrupy citrus.
farms.notable: José Ever Muñoz, farming since 2012 with his wife Fernanda, won the regional Copa de Oro and belongs to a respected Suaza growers’ association.

farms.name: Cerro Miraflores (group lot)
farms.note: Garzón, Huila, Colombia. A group lot from nine producers around Cerro Miraflores (Miraflores Hill) on the Cordillera Oriental, an area of páramo and alpine-tundra biome, who pooled their best Gesha cherries. Washed — grape, pineapple acidity, berry, lemon, wildflower aromatics and a silken texture.

farms.name: Alirio Muñoz (El Roble)
farms.note: Finca El Roble, Bruselas, Huila, Colombia. Alirio Muñoz and his wife Anayibe Ortiz oversee three farms totalling ~14 ha with their three children, growing Pink Bourbon, Bourbon Aji, Ombligón and more. A bright, clean washed Pink Bourbon — strawberry, blood orange, tropical florals.

farms.name: Divo Niño (group lot)
farms.people: Eugenio & José Cantillo, Edgar Garcia Ossa, Javier Cantillo, Yamir Anacona
farms.note: Suaza, Huila, Colombia. "Divine Child" — a five-producer group (via importer Osito) pooling Gesha so small farmers earn top price rather than losing it in a field blend. Coordinated picking, fermentation and drying yield a clean, juicy washed cup — peach, pear, golden apple, melon, honeysuckle.

farms.name: Nimrod Pineda
farms.note: Santa Bárbara, Honduras. Nimrod Pineda lost his father at 9 and helped his older brother run the farm; he started his own nursery at 20 and began producing in 2022, now 25. A rare Honduran washed SL-28 of real refinement — currant, blackberry, pluot, juicy citrus, full body and integrated florality.

farms.name: Ruber Izaguirre (El Pino)
farms.note: Finca El Pino, El Cedral, Las Vegas, Santa Bárbara, Honduras. Ruber Izaguirre, a third-generation grower, farms just 0.7 ha of Pacas and Parainema with his father Crecencio. A balanced, comforting washed Pacas — caramelised tamarind, maple, raisin, date, peach.

farms.name: Rumudamo Washing Station
farms.note: Arbegona woreda, Sidama, Ethiopia. The Arbegona Rumudamo washing station is managed by Asefa Dukamo Korma, with 600+ farmers delivering mostly 74112 and other heirloom varieties; the station placed 2nd and 3rd at the 2020 Ethiopia Cup of Excellence. A barrel-fermented washed lot — blackberry, raspberry, rose, milk chocolate, blood orange.
farms.notable: Placed 2nd and 3rd at the 2020 Ethiopia Cup of Excellence.

farms.name: Boku Sayisa Washing Station
farms.note: Hambela district, Western Guji, Ethiopia. Boku Sayisa ("Blessed Valley" in Amharic) draws cherry from ~565 high-altitude smallholders growing JARC, Dega and Kudhume landraces. A striking, very-high-grown washed community lot — blueberry, peony, sugar cane, stewed fruit, lychee and a long floral aftertaste.

farms.name: Udesa Fora Washing Station
farms.note: Sukke Chiga, Kercha district, Guji, Ethiopia. Udesa Fora ("resting under a tree") is managed by Mekonnen Beriso, with 300–400 farmers growing heirloom 74112 and 74158; 72-hour ferment, washed, 8–12 day drying. Velvety and bright — blackberry, plum, apricot, peach, delicate florals.

farms.name: Chelbessa Washing Station
farms.note: Gedeb, Gedeo, Ethiopia. The Chelbessa washing station draws on ~1,000 smallholders with tiny plots around 2,000 m, working Ethiopian heirloom landraces. A washed Gedeo benchmark — strawberry, nectarine, moon grape, passionfruit, with a rounded tea-like body and lingering candied-fruit florals.

farms.name: Keramo Washing Station
farms.note: Keramo Village, Bensa, Sidama, Ethiopia. A Daye Bensa-linked station (Asefa Dukamo) whose farmers hold Cup of Excellence titles for clarity and consistency. A sparkling, high-grown washed landrace — pink lemonade, deep berry, strawberry acidity, juniper, minerality and a sustained floral finish.
farms.notable: A Daye Bensa-linked station (Asefa Dukamo) whose farmers hold Cup of Excellence titles for clarity and consistency.

farms.name: Gititu Factory
farms.note: Kiambu County, ~47 km north-west of Nairobi, Kenya. Gititu Factory (one of seven under the Gititu Farmers Co-operative Society) brings in over a thousand smallholders, primarily growing SL-28. A clean AA washed separation — blackberry, currant, candied plum, goji, melon, citrus blossom, honey.

farms.name: Kathakwa Factory
farms.note: Manyatta division, Embu County, Kenya, on the slopes of Mount Kenya. Kathakwa Factory (est. 1964, 1,000+ members) produced this bright AA separation of mostly SL-28 and SL-34. Round and juicy with melon character and lively acidity — a standout subscriber lot.

farms.name: Kiamugumo Factory
farms.note: Ngariama, Nyangeni, Kirinyaga County, Kenya, on the slopes of Mount Kenya. Kiamugumo Factory processes smallholder cherry with water from the Kii river; glacier-fed rivers keep the area at a privileged 55–75°F. A sparkling washed field blend — orange, blackcurrant, lemon-lime, rounded cherry and dried-fruit undertones.

farms.name: Gachatha Factory
farms.note: Nyeri County, Kenya. Gachatha Coffee Factory (est. 1963, ~1,542 members) sits on 392 acres between Muthuaini, Thiriku, Gachenge and Kianjau villages, treating all wastewater in soaking pits. A washed Nyeri standout — dried berry, mandarin orange, cane-juice viscosity, sharp clean complexity.

farms.name: Kayu Factory
farms.note: Kangema division, Murang'a County, Kenya. Kayu Coffee Factory (est. 1964) sits on 7 acres serving Kayu, Njumbi, Rwathia and Mihuti villages (~1,191 members) — the oldest factory near the area's tea zones. A glossy, multilayered washed cup — raspberry, date, rose, currant, black-raspberry, lemon-lime.

farms.name: Gaharo Hill (Long Miles)
farms.note: Gaharo Hill, Gitega province, Burundi. Where the Long Miles Coffee Project began (founded by Ben & Kristy Carlson); cherries are processed at Bukeye, the first station Long Miles built, at the foot of Gaharo Hill — its bricks made from valley clay by the same farmers who now deliver there. Washed Bourbon — marigold, coffee blossom, currant, oolong, citrus.

farms.name: Giku Hill (Long Miles)
farms.note: Giku Hill, Kayanza, Burundi — a remote hill near the Kibira Forest (Burundi's only indigenous rainforest), reached by crossing two rivers and two province borders, where communities have little access to clean water or electricity. A Long Miles project; a clean honey-processed Bourbon — nectarine, Rainier cherry, carob, baking spice, red tea.

farms.name: Ninga Washing Station
farms.note: Ninga Hill, Muramvya, Burundi. Ninga washing station has its own borehole; processing includes a single dry ferment, traditional foot-"dancing" to loosen mucilage, density grading and a soak. A washed Bourbon field blend that evolves hot-to-cool — apricot, persimmon, meringue, baking spice, berry tea, clean citrus.

farms.name: Remera Washing Station
farms.note: Kayanza, Burundi. Remera washing station is led by three friends — Picasso, Zephyrin and Jeremiah — who unusually are also farmers at scale, having planted 38,000+ trees and built an innovative direct supply chain. 12h wet + 12h dry cement-tank ferment, 20–25 day drying. Washed Red Bourbon — pink lady apple, whipped cream, blackberry, blueberry, silky florals.

farms.name: Bumba Washing Station
farms.note: Bumba Hill, Kayanza, Burundi — a remote community reached by crossing three rivers and two province borders, beyond clean water and electricity, where coffee competes with corn and beans. A clean, tart washed Bourbon — rhubarb, apple crisp, silky plum, pear and a singing acidity over a firm structure.

farms.name: Hacienda La Papaya (Juan Peña)
farms.note: Saraguro, Loja, Ecuador. Hacienda La Papaya is the pioneer of specialty coffee in Saraguro — founded ~2009 by Juan Peña, a former rose farmer and agricultural engineer who began exporting to the US in 2014 and has since become one of Ecuador's most internationally recognised producers (repeated top-three Cup of Excellence finishes and a fixture on the barista-competition circuit). The ~28-acre farm runs as a research center: drip irrigation and soil sensors across all plots, an iterating nursery, and obsessive brix/fermentation/drying control. ~80% of its formally-employed workforce are women from the Saraguro region. Grows curated Sidra, Mejorado (Typica Mejorado) and Gesha; fully washed lots prized for clarity.
farms.notable: Hacienda La Papaya is the pioneer of specialty coffee in Saraguro — founded ~2009 by Juan Peña, a former rose farmer and agricultural.

farms.name: La Salvaje (Fabio Jr Caballero)
farms.note: Marcala / Santa Ana, Honduras. The personal project of Fabio Jr Caballero — grandson of the legendary Don Fabio Caballero — whose washed Geisha took 1st place at the 2025 Honduras Cup of Excellence on the farm's first full commercial harvest. Elegant florals, vibrant citrus, exceptional clarity.
farms.notable: The personal project of Fabio Jr Caballero — grandson of the legendary Don Fabio Caballero — whose washed Geisha took 1st place at the Cup of Excellence.

farms.name: Las Virginias (Don Fabio Caballero)
farms.note: Marcala, Honduras. A Caballero-family farm planted within the natural landscape; multiple Cup of Excellence wins across the family. Washed Geisha, mechanically demucilaged and slow low-temperature dried up to 70h — plum, pomelo, brown sugar.
farms.notable: Multiple Cup of Excellence wins across the family.

farms.name: Plan del Hoyo (de Sola family)
farms.note: Apaneca-Ilamatepec, El Salvador. A de Sola family farm worked since 1957, latterly focused on coffee with strong Cup of Excellence results. Washed Geisha — 16h jute-sack ferment, ~16 days on brick patios — red apple, tangelo, yellow peach.
farms.notable: A de Sola family farm worked since 1957, latterly focused on coffee with strong Cup of Excellence results.

farms.name: Los Pirineos (Baraona family)
farms.note: Tecapa-Chinameca, Usulután, El Salvador. One of El Salvador's most iconic farms, run by the Baraona family for 130+ years atop the Tecapa volcano; legacy of the late Gilberto Baraona continued by son Diego. Renowned for Pacamara and meticulous CM/auction lots.
farms.notable: Renowned for Pacamara and meticulous CM/auction lots.

farms.name: Emilio Lopez (Cuatro M / Las Mercedes)
farms.note: Apaneca, El Salvador. Emilio López Díaz of the Cuatro M Cafés / Las Mercedes group is a leading Salvadoran producer-exporter known for clean, expressive lots. Washed Geisha.

farms.name: Bridazul (El Árbol mill)
farms.note: Dipilto, Nueva Segovia, Nicaragua. Bridazul works with smallholders across Nueva Segovia, centralising processing at the El Árbol mill with agronomy support and fair pricing. The "Supernatural" process gives deep purple grape, plum jam and Black Forest character.

farms.name: Elkin Guzman (Finca El Mirador)
farms.note: Huila, Colombia. Elkin Guzmán manages Finca El Mirador (70+ years of family history) with an agricultural-engineering background and an experimental, fermentation-focused approach — one of Huila's most exciting sources. Honey-processed Striped Red Bourbon and Tabi.

farms.name: Sebastian Ramirez (Finca El Placer)
farms.note: Calarcá, Quindío, Colombia. Fourth-generation producer who left law studies to transform the family farm El Placer into a "seed to cup" hub for experimental processing (long CO₂-injected anaerobic ferments, lactic inoculation). Known for boundary-pushing Pink Bourbon, Sidra and Geisha.

farms.name: Nestor Lasso (Finca El Diviso)
farms.note: Bruselas / Pitalito, Huila, Colombia. Brothers Nestor and Adrian Lasso with friend Jhoan Vergara built El Diviso into one of Colombia's most forward-thinking ventures — advanced thermal-shock and anaerobic fermentations, world-competition recognition. Sidra and Ombligón standouts.
farms.notable: Brothers Nestor and Adrian Lasso with friend Jhoan Vergara built El Diviso into one of Colombia's most forward-thinking ventures —.

farms.name: Henry Bonilla (Finca El Arrayan)
farms.note: Huila, Colombia. Henry Bonilla took over Finca El Arrayán after his father's passing and pushed into rare varieties and experimental fermentation. His "Flor de Jamaica" (hibiscus) Pink Bourbon pre-ferments fruit into a concentrated juice added during processing — hibiscus, sakura, lollipop.

farms.name: Jhonatan Gasca (Finca Bella Vista)
farms.note: Bella Vista, Bruselas, Huila, Colombia. Jhonatan Gasca farms Finca Bella Vista with his brother Johan, known for bold modern profiles and meticulous post-harvest work used in barista competitions. Thermal-Shock Natural Pacamara — cherry liqueur, Kyoho grape, dark chocolate.

farms.name: El Encanto (Faiver Fernández)
farms.note: Paicol, Huila, Colombia. A family lot ("The Enchantment") grown by Faiver Fernández — washed Pink Bourbon, 26h airtight steel-tank ferment, 12-day sun drying. Silky bodied with nectarine, red berries and brown sugar.

farms.name: Thiago Corsi (Fazenda Rodomunho)
farms.note: Cerrado Mineiro, Brazil. Fourth-generation Corsi family farm; Thiago is recognised for regenerative agriculture (soil health, biodiversity). His natural Gesha uses slow cold fermentation at 17–18°C — starfruit, loquat, cranberry.

farms.name: Santuario Sul (Luiz Paulo Pereira)
farms.note: Carmo de Minas, Mantiqueira de Minas, Brazil. Founded 2017 next to the renowned Irmãs Pereira farm, Santuario Sul grows 30+ exotic varieties as a hub of Brazilian innovation. Natural Geisha — strawberry, nectarine, sugar cane.
farms.notable: Founded 2017 next to the renowned Irmãs Pereira farm, Santuario Sul grows 30+ exotic varieties as a hub of Brazilian innovation.

farms.name: Ana Melva Sampertegui Sanchez
farms.note: Alto Tambillo, San Ignacio, Cajamarca, Peru. Ana Melva Sampertegui Sánchez has farmed her 1-ha plot for nearly half her life, using biological pest controls (Beauveria bassiana) and selective pruning. Washed Caturra & Bourbon (24h cherry + 48h parchment ferment) — Bravo apple, berries, Darjeeling.

farms.name: Roxana Chambi (Finca San Lorenzo)
farms.note: Caranavi, La Paz, Bolivia. A 5-ha farm founded 2019 by Roxana Chambi; cherries handpicked at peak ripeness and naturally sun-dried. Natural Catuai — Nashi pear, prune, fudge.

farms.name: Finca Aurora Machaca (Jhony Machaca)
farms.note: Caranavi, La Paz, Bolivia. Established 2011 by Jhony Machaca; 3.5 ha of coffee (4,000 Red Catuai trees) on a 9-ha farm, hand-harvested June–September, mostly raised-bed natural dried. Washed lot — red currant, apricot, brown sugar.

farms.name: Mil Cumbres
farms.people: Mario Fonseca & Arturo Klein (Ratibor Hartmann, production)
farms.note: Cordillera, near Volcán, Chiriquí, Panama, on the slopes of Barú Volcano. "A thousand hills" — a former cattle pasture turned specialty farm (25 of 60 ha in coffee), reached via a reactivated timber trail. Natural Geisha — candied orange, red plum, longan, oolong.

farms.name: Koke Washing Station
farms.note: Koke, Yirgacheffe, Ethiopia. Garden-coffee smallholders deliver to the Koke station; the "Violet" profile is dried whole on raised beds 18–30 days. Natural landrace — lilac, sapphire grape, papaya.

farms.name: Chelbesa Wet Mill (Chelle)
farms.note: Gedeb woreda, Gedeo, Ethiopia. The "Chelle" lot from the Chelbesa wet mill — 36–72h concrete-tank ferment, 8–12 day shaded raised-bed drying. Washed landrace — jasmine, nectarine, sugarcane, tea-like.

farms.name: Dumerso Washing Station
farms.note: Gedeo, Yirgacheffe, Ethiopia. A renowned washing station; hundreds of smallholders deliver landrace garden coffee, fermented underwater 36–48h and dried up to two weeks. Apricot, orange zest, jasmine.
farms.notable: A renowned Yirgacheffe washing station celebrated for its bright, floral, citrus-driven lots.

farms.name: Worka Sakaro
farms.note: Gedeo, Yirgacheffe, Ethiopia. A 120-ha high-altitude source pushing carbonic maceration in a washed-dominant region — 10-day CO₂ anaerobic ferment. CM Natural landrace — black plum, red grape, chocolate ganache.

farms.name: Gargari (Gutity, Kochere)
farms.note: Gutity, Kochere, Yirgacheffe, Ethiopia. A prized Kochere microzone with dry days and cool nights; the "Amber" lot is CO₂-flushed anaerobic, cherries at 20–22 Brix, slow thick-layer drying. Lychee, Kyoho grape, wine gums.

farms.name: Hafursa Washing Station
farms.note: Hafursa, Gedeo, Yirgacheffe, Ethiopia. Backyard-garden smallholders; washed, 12–24h fermentation, rinsed in channels, up to 15 days on raised beds. Mandarin, white floral, sugarcane.

farms.name: Nansebo Station (West Arsi)
farms.note: West Arsi, Ethiopia. Backyard-garden heirloom at up to 2,300 m; natural, raised-bed dried 18–30 days with triple hand- and colour-sorting. Cherry, red grapefruit, molasses.

farms.name: Sharbo Maro
farms.note: Mokonisa, Wenago, Gedeo, Ethiopia. At 80, Sharbo Maro is among Mokonisa's most respected farmers, working 7 ha (large by local standards) with son Daniel; washed-only since 2015. 48h underwater ferment with daily water changes, shade then raised-bed dried. Peach, lemon sherbet, frangipani.

farms.name: Abebe Cherfo
farms.note: Rakko, Kochere, Yirgacheffe, Ethiopia. A single-farmer lot from Abebe Cherfo of the Jabanto Farmers Group (supported by G Broad Trading & Condesa Co.Lab), near Rakko Mountain; organic-certified washed. 48h soak in fresh mountain water, raised-bed dried. Tangelo, grapes, brown sugar.

farms.name: Karatina Factory
farms.note: Barichu, Nyeri County, Kenya, southern slopes of Mt Kenya. Part of the Barichu FCS (~1,450 smallholders) on red volcanic soils; traditional river-water washed, overnight ferment, 7–15 days on raised beds. Mulberry, dark plum, sugarcane.

farms.name: Kagumoini Factory
farms.note: Murang'a County, Kenya. Part of the Kamacharia FCS (1,300+ members) on red volcanic soils; classic Kenyan washed processing. Yellow peach, red berries, caramel.

farms.name: Thageini Wet Mill
farms.note: Nyeri, Kenya. Part of the Aguthi FCS (~2,000 producers); the "Amber" lot is a rare Kenyan carbonic maceration — ripest cherries at 20–22 Brix, 8-day CO₂ ferment. Blackberry, cherry reduction, toffee.

farms.name: Kibingo Washing Station
farms.note: Kayanza commune, northern Burundi (~1,893 m). Kibingo serves 1,700+ growers across 18 hills, organised in groups of 30; 10 ferment tanks, 2 soaking tanks, 165 raised beds. Washed Red Bourbon — Bravo apple, blackberry, molasses.

farms.name: Yinhui Coffee Estate (Hui Li)
farms.note: Guangbiezhongzhai, Menglian, Pu'er, Yunnan, China. A 2,300-acre estate of ~200 growers founded by Hui Li, who trained with Nestlé agronomists and returned home to lift her village out of poverty through strict quality control. Catimor (T7963/T5175/PT688) and Yellow Bourbon; natural processing.

farms.name: Cimizhu Estate (Po ZHA)
farms.note: Lisuo, Ximeng, Pu'er, Yunnan, China. Founded by Po ZHA, who left banking in 2007 to build a quality-focused estate paying villagers on cherry quality; 2nd place Unwashed at the 2022 Best of Yunnan competition. Anaerobic Natural Catimor — bayberry, umeboshi, dark chocolate.

farms.name: Xiuyuan Coffee Estate
farms.note: Menglian, Pu'er, Yunnan, China. Managed by 550 smallholders across the 7,373-ha Menglian growing area; primarily Catimor. Anaerobic Natural (48–72h sealed ferment) — red plum, candied hawthorn, fudge.

farms.name: Xingang Tea & Coffee (Mei ZHAO)
farms.note: Fuyan, near Menglian, Pu'er, Yunnan, China. Established 2006; second-generation producer Mei ZHAO has guided 2,700 farmers via a cooperative. Long anaerobic-natural (480h ferment + pile fermentation) Catimor T5715 — red grape, apricot, Pedro Ximénez.

farms.name: Slope Coffee Estate
farms.note: Xiding, Menghai, Xishuangbanna, Yunnan, China. In a Blang ethnic-group village, Slope grows Purple Caturra ("Purple Leaf"), a natural Caturra mutation developed by the Dehong Tropical Research Institute. K72 washed (spring-water floated, double ferment) — pear, nectarine, macadamia.

farms.name: El Higuerón (Ever Gamboa, Finca Los Robles)
farms.note: San Lorenzo, Nariño, Colombia. Ever Gamboa worked for years as an "andariego" (travelling picker) before founding Finca Los Robles with his brother six years ago, moving into specialty cultivars. Washed Pink Bourbon — key lime pie, white peach, nectar.

farms.name: La Joya (Chachagüí)
farms.note: Chachagüí, Nariño, Colombia. A high-altitude Nariño lot in a municipality with deep Quillacinga indigenous heritage. Natural Caturra/Variedad Colombia — pineapple, raspberry, apricot compote.

farms.name: El Palmichal (Atilano)
farms.note: Génova, Quindío, Colombia. Atilano, a third-generation grower (his father came from Antioquia in the 1950s), farms 70 ha of exclusively Castillo with soil-analysis-led organic practices and Guamo/Nogal shade. Washed — berry jam, raisin, caramel.

farms.name: Sebastian Gomez (La Divisa)
farms.note: Circasia, Quindío, Colombia. Sebastián Gómez produces meticulous microlots at Finca La Divisa — 30h underwater fermentation, temperature-controlled drying. Washed Caturra — apple, nectarine, brown sugar.

farms.name: Daniel Calderon (Finca El Mirador, Algeciras)
farms.note: Algeciras, Huila, Colombia. Daniel Calderón Enciso farms 6 ha (≈3 in coffee) at Finca El Mirador, a partner producer of Azahar in a municipality where coffee shares the land with cane and cacao. Natural Caturra/Colombia — raisin, umeboshi, caramel.

farms.name: Luz Helena Salazar (La Leona)
farms.note: La Leona, Armenia, Quindío, Colombia. Luz Helena Salazar produces a carefully sorted microlot — strict ripeness criteria, floated and hand-sorted, 30h underwater fermentation. Washed Caturra — Fuji apple, plum, brown sugar.

farms.name: Finca El Guamo (INVERFINCA)
farms.note: Tacuba, Apaneca-Ilamatepec, El Salvador. INVERFINCA (Fernando Alfaro & Jose Enrique Gutierrez) — four generations of Salvadoran expertise — farms 45 ha beside Parque Nacional El Imposible. Natural Caturra — blackberry, miso, chocolate.

farms.name: El Limo (Jose Enrique Gutierrez)
farms.note: Apaneca-Ilamatepec, El Salvador. Second-generation producer Jose Enrique Gutierrez bought his first farm in 2007 with a specialty mindset after his family left coffee during the civil war. Anaerobic Natural San Pacho (Bourbon) — purple grape, dark chocolate, Negroni.

farms.name: El Pozo (Isaac Luna)
farms.note: Chalatenango, El Salvador ("valley of water and sand"), the country's coolest, northernmost region. Isaac Luna farms several small plots and was the first locally to process honey coffee. Natural Pacamara — ~20 days on raised beds.

farms.name: Los Chorros (Virgilio Martínez)
farms.note: San Pedro Necta, Huehuetenango, Guatemala. Virgilio Martínez farms 6.25 manzanas of mixed varieties; same-day depulping, 24–38h dry ferment, multiple washes, 3.5–6 days on concrete patios. Apricot, red apple, caramel.

farms.name: Roger Dominguez
farms.note: La Paz, Marcala, Honduras. A young producer who inherited family farms in the early 1990s and refined them for quality — three 3–5 ha parcels of Bourbon, Catuai and Caturra. Natural Catuai Rojo — red plum, orange, chocolate.

farms.name: Natcha Coffee (Natanael Charis)
farms.note: West Java, Indonesia (highlands of Mt Pangrango, Mt Halu, Mt Cikurai), with a processing facility in Aceh, North Sumatra. Natanael Charis — "Natcha" — is one of Indonesia's most innovative producers, known for award-winning controlled carbonic maceration with selected wine yeasts and thermal-shock techniques that elevate clarity and structure.
farms.notable: One of Indonesia's most awarded producers, known for carbonic-maceration innovation.

farms.name: Harusuke Washing Station
farms.note: Haru village, Gedeo, Yirgacheffe, Ethiopia. A washing station established 2017 by Abreham Mengiste, serving 2,820 smallholders. Washed heirloom — candied lemon, white peach, jasmine tea; clean and delicate.

farms.name: Hachi Project
farms.people: Diego Bermúdez, Matheus Antonaci & Allan Hartmann
farms.note: Jurutungo & Renacimiento, Volcán, Chiriquí, Panama. A venture by Diego Bermúdez (of El Paraíso fame) with Matheus Antonaci and Allan Hartmann, merging biotechnology and terroir — proprietary "Enzyflow" and "Symbiotic" fermentations (transferring aromatics between varieties via donor mosto). Boundary-pushing Gesha and Caturra.
farms.notable: Diego Bermúdez's biotech-driven Panama venture pushing fermentation science.

farms.name: Brim Coffee House (Da Lat)
farms.note: Da Lat, Lam Dong, Vietnam. Brim works with ethnic-minority farmers in the Da Lat highlands growing Catimor, hand-picked and sun-dried on hand-woven bamboo nets ~30 days. Known for distinctive processing — bamboo-dried naturals and a 100-hour lavender co-ferment.

farms.name: Barokah Kerinci (Koerintji Barokah Bersama)
farms.note: Gunung Tujuh, Kerinci, Sumatra, Indonesia. The Koerintji Barokah Bersama Cooperative (320 farmers, founded 2017, led by Triyono) sits at the foot of Mount Kerinci with nine UPH collection centres. Anaerobic Natural — mulled wine, plum reduction, sultana.

farms.name: Asman Arianto (Ribang Gayo Musara)
farms.note: Aceh Tengah, North Sumatra, Indonesia. Asman Arianto migrated to Aceh in 1998 and founded the Ribang Gayo Musara Cooperative (2018, 350+ members) delivering to Pantan Musara. Natural process — ruby grapefruit, spiced cola, palm sugar.

farms.name: Asman Gayo Mill (Takengon)
farms.note: Pegasing, Takengon, Aceh, Sumatra, Indonesia. A small mill serving Pantan Musara producers who rebuilt after displacement; unusually for Sumatra it produces washed (and natural) coffees, with recirculated wash water. Pomelo, apricot, Ceylon tea.

farms.name: Jaya Tani (Jernih Jaya, Kerinci)
farms.note: Jernih Jaya, Kerinci, Jambi, Sumatra, Indonesia. A lot from the Koerintji Barokah Bersama Cooperative (Triyono; 380 members across 200+ ha) in the rainforest highlands below Mount Kerinci. Anaerobic Natural — golden kiwi, cherry, fresh figs.

farms.name: Suke Quto Farm (Tesfaye Bekele)
farms.note: Odo Shakisso, Guji, Ethiopia. Founded in 2000 by Ato Tesfaye Bekele, a former government natural-resource official who left his post to plant coffee after the 1997–99 Guji bushfires destroyed ~5,000 acres of forest — restoring the land while creating livelihoods. A semi-forest, certified Organic & Rainforest Alliance farm (two farms plus a washing station serving 170–290 outgrowers), regarded as one of the pioneers that put Guji on the specialty map; a long partnership with importer Trabocca. Unusually for Ethiopia the varieties are clearly identified — Kurume and Wolicho — grown under native shade with leaf-mulch fertilisation. Classic clean Guji cup: floral, stone fruit, citrus, tea-like.
farms.notable: A semi-forest, certified Organic & Rainforest Alliance farm (two farms plus a washing station serving 170–290 outgrowers), regarded as.

farms.name: Ana Sora (Israel Degfa)
farms.note: Guji, Oromia, Ethiopia (east of Yirgacheffe, near Yirgalem). A ~250-ha private estate — rare in Ethiopia — founded in 2013 by Israel Degfa, the second-generation producer behind Kerchanshe Trading (20+ washing stations). Among the highest-grown coffees in the world at up to 2,350 m; the extreme altitude slows cherry maturation for intense sweetness and acidity. Israel grew up in coffee (father a mill manager, mother selling beans at a bus stop) and reinvests via the Nakala sustainability arm. Washed and natural 11/714 landrace.
farms.notable: Among the highest-grown coffees in the world, at up to roughly 2,350 metres in Guji, Ethiopia.

farms.name: Dimtu Washing Station (Hambela)
farms.note: Hambela woreda, Guji, Ethiopia. A washing station in the Kerchanshe (Israel Degfa) network, known for perfumed, floral washed lots with concentrated berry, dark chocolate and baking-spice depth in naturals.

farms.name: Hunkute Cooperative
farms.note: Hunkute, Sidama, Ethiopia. A large, long-established cooperative on the Sidama plateau, a benchmark washed-Sidama name — clean, citrus-and-floral with classic black-tea elegance.

farms.name: Konga Washing Station
farms.note: Konga, Yirgacheffe (Gedeo), Ethiopia. A celebrated Yirgacheffe kebele/station, one of the classic reference points for floral, citrus, jasmine-driven washed Yirgacheffe.
farms.notable: A celebrated Yirgacheffe kebele/station, one of the classic reference points for floral, citrus, jasmine-driven washed Yirgacheffe.

farms.name: Chelelektu Washing Station
farms.note: Chelelektu, Kochere, Yirgacheffe, Ethiopia. A renowned Kochere station; both washed and natural lots prized for stone fruit, citrus and intense florals.
farms.notable: A renowned Kochere washing station known for delicate, tea-like Yirgacheffe character.

farms.name: Karogoto Factory
farms.note: Tekangu FCS, Nyeri, Kenya. One of the standout Nyeri factories (Tekangu = Tegu/Karogoto/Ngunguru), classic blackcurrant-and-citrus washed Kenyan SL-28/SL-34.

farms.name: Kiandu Factory
farms.note: Nyeri, Kenya. A well-regarded Nyeri factory producing dense, high-altitude washed lots with the region's signature blackcurrant, grapefruit and winey acidity.

farms.name: Finca Tamana
farms.note: Pitalito, Huila, Colombia. A widely celebrated farm popularised through exporter Pergamino and roasters worldwide; Elías Roa's meticulous plot-by-plot management and soil work made Tamana a reference Colombian washed coffee — caramel, red fruit, balanced and sweet.
farms.notable: A widely celebrated Colombian farm popularised through the exporter Pergamino and roasters worldwide.

farms.name: Las Margaritas (Granja La Esperanza)
farms.note: Trujillo, Valle del Cauca, Colombia. One of Café Granja La Esperanza's farms (Herrera family), where some of Colombia's earliest and most awarded Geisha plantings were established — a benchmark Colombian Gesha source.

farms.name: Finca Argentina
farms.note: Santa Ana (Ilamatepec/Apaneca), El Salvador. A historic farm associated with Aída Batlle, one of El Salvador's most influential producers and an early champion of meticulous picking and processing. Classic Salvadoran Bourbon — red fruit, caramel, citrus.

farms.name: Hacienda Cañas Verdes (Abu Coffee)
farms.note: Cañas Verdes district, Boquete, Chiriquí, Panama. A ~11-ha farm on high-organic volcanic soil (pH 5.0–6.0, ~3,500 mm rainfall), producing Geisha and Catuai. 'Abu' (short for abuelo) honours José Guillermo Luttrell Tedman, the third-generation producer whose grand-uncle bought the original Geisha seed in 1971; current producer José Luttrell has earned multiple Best of Panama auction placements in only a few years (a Natural Geisha placed 11th). Known for double-phase anaerobic washed processing — jasmine, lychee, tangerine, chocolate and mixed berries.
farms.notable: Multiple Best of Panama auction placements in only a few years.

farms.name: Uchangi Estate
farms.note: Chikmagalur, Karnataka, India, at roughly 900–1,200 m. A shade-grown estate in India’s oldest coffee region whose lots have reached specialty roasters abroad, including experimental yeast-fermented coffees. (Further detail on ownership and varieties to be confirmed.)

seedTastings.notes: Textbook washed Gesha. Jasmine on the nose, bergamot mid-palate, silky tea body. Lingering florals.
Big tropical ferment character — mango, lychee. Honeyed finish. Edge of boozy but stays clean.
